archetype: Orbital Debris Conjunction Assessment & Collision Avoidance (CCA) Trajectory Optimization Engine
domain: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) Satellite Constellation Operations
description: A deterministic, high-frequency computing system that ingests Two-Line Element (TLE) and OMM data to predict collision probabilities between active satellites and orbital debris, automatically generating and validating Delta-V maneuver plans for autonomous satellite constellations. It is technically demanding due to the extreme sensitivity of orbital mechanics propagation, the need for sub-millimeter precision in position forecasting, and the strict requirement to operate within the 12-24 hour decision window mandated by international conjunction assessment protocols.
rules: MUST adhere to CCSDS (Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems) OMM (Orbit Mean-Elements Message) standards for all incoming ephemeris data exchange to ensure interoperability with US Space Command and international partners.

MUST implement Gaussian Smearing algorithms to calculate Conjunction Data Message (CDM) probability of collision (Pc) with a minimum false-negative tolerance of <10^-7 to prevent catastrophic loss of multi-million dollar assets.
MUST integrate real-time telemetry constraints from the satellite's propulsion system (specific engine Isp and fuel mass limits) to ensure generated avoidance maneuvers are physically executable.

MUST support deterministic time propagation using high-precision models (e.g., SGP4/SDP4) with atmospheric drag coefficient updates sourced from real-time solar flux and geomagnetic index (Kp/Ap) data streams.

MUST enforce strict audit trails of all maneuver decisions and rejected trajectories to satisfy ISO 24113 (Space Systems — Space Debris Mitigation Requirements) compliance for insurance and liability purposes.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use general-purpose machine learning models for trajectory prediction without physics-informed neural networks, as they lack the interpretability and fail-safe guarantees required for life-safety critical space operations.

DO NOT rely on asynchronous, eventually consistent databases for state updates; the system requires strong consistency to ensure the latest orbital state is used for the immediate next collision probability calculation.

DO NOT decouple the maneuver generation logic from the propulsion hardware model; generating a 5m/s burn plan without verifying fuel mass availability leads to mission failure due to inability to execute the command.
key_technical_challenges: Managing the computational load of propagating millions of debris objects against a growing constellation of active satellites every 15 minutes while maintaining microsecond latency for critical alerts.

Resolving the 'debt spiral' where frequent small avoidance maneuvers deplete fuel faster than the satellite's expected lifetime, requiring complex multi-objective optimization to balance collision risk vs. propellant budget.

Handling the 'uncertainty ellipsoid' expansion over time, where small initial measurement errors in debris position explode exponentially, requiring adaptive covariance matrix regularization techniques.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Provides memory safety guarantees essential for flight-critical software while delivering the raw performance needed for intensive matrix operations in orbital propagation.
recommended_stack.framework: Actix Web — A high-performance, async-oriented web framework capable of handling the massive throughput of telemetry streams and CDM ingestion from multiple ground stations.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL extension) — Optimized for storing and querying massive time-series telemetry data and historical orbital elements with efficient time-windowed aggregations.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis Cluster — For rapid access to current satellite state vectors and active mission parameters to minimize latency in the critical decision loop.
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Pulsar — Supports geo-redundant multi-tenancy and ensures reliable delivery of high-priority conjunction alerts across distributed ground station networks.

compliance_flags: FAA Office of Commercial Space Transportation Licensing Requirements
